scan.target: amset (Ab-initio Scattering and Transport): electronic transport from first principles, as used by AtomisticSkills (arXiv 2605.24002)
scan.stage: SCAN (catalog only; an encode agent owns omai/, docs/data, map/)
scan.purpose: Seed a future electronic-transport domain. Catalog the transport quantities amset produces (electronic conductivity, Seebeck, electronic thermal conductivity, mobility, per-mechanism scattering rates), the cross-domain DFT inputs it consumes, dimensions with exponent vectors, and the lattice-vs-electronic thermal-conductivity distinction.
scan.source_package: amset 0.5.1 (pip download --no-deps; wheel unzipped to /tmp/amsetsrc/amset_pkg). amset is NOT importable in the miniconda base env (No module named 'amset'). AtomisticSkills runs it in the atomate2-agent env with both atomate2 and amset installed.
scan.atomistic_skills_usage: Single skill: mat-dft-electronic-transport. It reaches amset ONLY through atomate2's VaspAmsetMaker (atomate2.vasp.flows.amset). No direct amset API is invoked by the skill; the skill builds the VASP+AMSET DAG and jobflow-remote submits it. amset outputs are parsed 'natively using standard amset.plot utilities' (SKILL.md), so the amset transport JSON is the true output contract.
scan.graph_snapshot: docs/data/graph.json read at scan time: 73 nodes. NONE of amset's transport quantities is present (no ElectricalConductivity, Seebeck, Mobility, ScatteringRate, ElectronicThermalConductivity). Three cross-domain INPUTS amset needs ARE already on the map: DielectricTensor, BornCharges, Frequency (phonon), plus ElasticConstants. The map's ThermalConductivity[*] family is LATTICE thermal conductivity; amset's kappa_e is the ELECTRONIC contribution: same dimension, different carrier. The task noted matcalc/ASE encode may land records 145-147 while running.
scan.review_snapshot_2026-07-10: DEEP-REVIEW re-read: map/log.jsonl is 144 records; docs/data/graph.json is 73 nodes; the matcalc/ASE encode had NOT landed (no records 145-147). CORRECTION: the lattice ThermalConductivity[*] family has 9 nodes on the current graph (2 bte_solver + 7 transport_model), NOT 11 as stated elsewhere in this catalog. DielectricTensor, BornCharges, Frequency, ElasticConstants, BandGap, Voltage all confirmed present.
scan.boltztrap2_note: amset's transport tensors (sigma, seebeck, kappa) come from BoltzTraP2's calc_Onsager_coefficients over the Fermi integrals (transport.py:6,123,186); band interpolation is BoltzTraP2's smoothed-Fourier fite/sphere (bandstructure.py:10,53 class Interpolator; boltztrap.py:4 FFTc/FFTev). amset's contribution over bare BoltzTraP2 is the momentum-relaxation-time scattering (ADP/IMP/PIE/POP) replacing the constant relaxation time.

transport_tensor_structure.grid: Every transport quantity is a function of (doping, temperature). amset stores each as a full (n_doping, n_temperature, 3, 3) tensor (data.py: seebeck/kappa/sigma np.zeros(n_t_size + (3,3)); to_data() writes the upper triangle xx,xy,xz,yy,yz,zz per (n,t) row, data.py:464-489).
transport_tensor_structure.spectrum_layer_answer: The transport tensors are FULL ARRAYS over the (doping, T) grid, not scalars. This is the same spectrum-layer question the thermal domain answered for kappa(T): ingest the (doping, T, 3, 3) tensor via the representation/discretization layer, with doping and T as the grid axes. Scalar reduction is ONLY a reporting convenience: _log_results_summary uses tensor_average (isotropic trace/3) for the log table (run.py:588-590). The GaAs example quotes a single scalar mobility per carrier at 300 K, but that is a reduction of the tensor at one (doping, T) point.
transport_tensor_structure.doping_sign_convention: defaults.yaml:8 'positive doping concentrations indicate p-type doping, negative is n-type'. Doping is a carrier concentration in cm^-3 (input) internally converted to bohr^-3 (data.py:467 * cm_to_bohr**3; log converts back * (1/bohr_to_cm)**3, run.py:576). The GaAs skill sets doping=(1e16,1e17,1e18) cm^-3 and temperatures=(300.,400.) K (generate_inputs.py:45-46).

dimension_derivations.base_axis_order: (M, L, T, Th, N, I, J) per omai/operator/dimensions.py:26 _BASE = ('M','L','T','Th','N','I','J'). Verified programmatically against the map's own Dimension objects.

seebeck.serving_unit: microvolt per kelvin (muV/K); source multiplies raw V/K by 1e6 (transport.py:191 'seebeck *= 1e6'), log header 'S [uV/K]' (run.py:583)
seebeck.derivation: S = V/K. Volt (VOLTAGE node) = M L^2 T^-3 I^-1. Per kelvin adds Th^-1. So S = (M=1, L=2, T=-3, Th=-1, N=0, I=-1, J=0).
seebeck.verified: TRUE (matches task-supplied (1,2,-3,-1,0,-1,0); confirmed by hand and programmatically from dimensions.VOLTAGE).

mobility.serving_unit: cm^2/(V.s); conversion in source: uc = 0.01/(e_si*carrier_conc*(1/bohr_to_cm)**3); mobility = sigma * uc (transport.py:133-135), log header 'mu [cm^2/Vs]' (run.py:583)
mobility.verified: TRUE. The task's alternative guess (0,0,1,0,0,1,-1) is WRONG (it is not L T^-1 I J^-1); the correct answer is (M=-1, T=2, I=1), confirmed by hand and programmatically.

electrical_conductivity_electronic.serving_unit: S/m (siemens per metre); log header 'sigma [S/m]' (run.py:583), to_data unit 'S/m' (data.py:484). NOT S/cm.
electrical_conductivity_electronic.verified: TRUE. IDENTICAL DIMENSION to the config-thermo scan's IonicConductivity (M=-1,L=-3,T=3,I=2). This is the conductivity-family finding: electronic sigma and ionic sigma share the exponent vector exactly.

electronic_thermal_conductivity.serving_unit: W/(m.K). Source field name electronic_thermal_conductivity (data.py:80,419,473); NOTE amset source itself flags the unit as unconfirmed: data.py:483 '# TODO: confirm unit of kappa' with header unit '?' (data.py:484). It comes from BoltzTraP2 calc_Onsager_coefficients (transport.py:186), whose kappa is W/(m.K).
electronic_thermal_conductivity.verified: TRUE. IDENTICAL DIMENSION to the map's lattice ThermalConductivity (dimensions.py:115 THERMAL_CONDUCTIVITY = (1,1,-3,-1,0,0,0)). The lattice-vs-electronic distinction is by carrier/label, NOT by dimension (see lattice_vs_electronic_kappa).

scattering_rate.serving_unit: s^-1 (plot/rates.py:22-26 label tau^-1, unit 's$^{-1}$'; lifetime is s)
scattering_rate.verified: TRUE (= dimensions.FREQUENCY). The rate is decomposed per mechanism (ADP, IMP, PIE, POP, ...); each mechanism has the same dimension. Rates add by Matthiessen (total lifetime = 1/sum_mechanisms rate, transport.py:101,163).

scattering_mechanisms.note: amset's momentum-relaxation-time mechanisms (each a class with a two/three-letter name). Per-mechanism scattering rates 1/s are the resolved spectrum; separate_mobility (defaults.yaml:56) additionally gives a per-mechanism mobility breakdown (run.py:615-624, 'Mobility breakdown by scattering mechanism, in cm^2/Vs').
scattering_mechanisms.auto: scattering_type: auto (defaults.yaml:6) picks the physical mechanisms (ADP/PIE/IMP/POP) from the supplied materials parameters. The GaAs example targets ADP + POP (README objective; POP + piezoelectric-limited).

cross_domain_inputs.finding: amset's compute_electronic_transport operator is genuinely CROSS-DOMAIN: three of its required material properties are quantities the map ALREADY carries as nodes, produced by the phonon/DFT-ground-state domains. This is the strongest cross-domain edge candidate seen: an electronic-transport operator whose scattering inputs come from the phonon and dielectric domains.

already_on_map.note: amset needs BOTH the static (eps_0) and high-frequency (eps_inf) dielectric constants for POP and PIE screening. The map's DielectricTensor is the clamped-ion eps_inf (high-frequency). The STATIC eps_0 (ion-clamped + ionic contribution) is a DISTINCT quantity amset also needs and the map does not yet carry separately: candidate note.

The polar optical phonon frequency omega_LO. amset takes a single effective POP frequency in THz (defaults.yaml:41; inelastic.py:92 *1e12*2pi). The map's phonon Frequency node (per-mode omega_qnu) is the source; the effective POP frequency is a reduction of the phonon spectrum. CROSS-DOMAIN edge: phonon-domain Frequency -> electronic-transport scattering input.

Born charges drive the LO-TO splitting that sets omega_LO and the polar (Frohlich) coupling underlying POP. amset takes pop_frequency and the two dielectrics rather than Z* directly, so BornCharges feed amset UPSTREAM, not as a direct amset argument. REVIEW CONFIRMED from atomate2: VaspAmsetMaker calls calculate_polar_phonon_frequency(structure, normalmode_frequencies, normalmode_eigenvecs, outcar['born']) (vasp/flows/amset.py:245-250) to reduce Born charges + normal modes into the single effective pop_frequency it passes to amset. Cross-domain provenance, not a direct input slot.
already_on_map.anchor: atomate2 vasp/flows/amset.py:245-250 calculate_polar_phonon_frequency; map node BornCharges

not_yet_on_map.note: Acoustic deformation potential, computed by VaspAmsetMaker's strained-band step (SKILL.md: 'deformation potential calculation'). Candidate new source node (DeformationPotential).

REVIEW CORRECTION: amset takes the FULL rank-4 elastic tensor, NOT a longitudinal-average scalar. cast_elastic_tensor (util.py:115) accepts a single number OR a 6x6 Voigt OR a 3x3x3x3 tensor and ALWAYS expands to the full (3,3,3,3) C_ijkl (a scalar becomes an isotropic tensor). The longitudinal modulus c_long is derived per q-direction at run time from the Christoffel construction get_christoffel_tensors (elastic.py:266-267, np.einsum('ijkl,ni,nl->njk')) + solve_christoffel_equation (elastic.py:180-183), NOT supplied as a scalar. atomate2 VaspAmsetMaker wires elastic.output.elastic_tensor.raw (flows/amset.py:290), the full raw tensor. So the natural cross-domain edge is the full rank-4 ElasticConstants tensor. Cross-domain: mechanical-domain ElasticConstants -> transport scattering input.
not_yet_on_map.anchor: util.py:115-140 cast_elastic_tensor; elastic.py:100,180-183,266-267; defaults.yaml:36; atomate2 vasp/flows/amset.py:290

not_yet_on_map.note: Piezoelectric stress tensor for PIE scattering. Candidate new source node (PiezoelectricConstant, dim C/m^2 = (L^-2, T, I) like SpontaneousPolarization).

The dense uniform band structure + wavefunction coefficients (wavefunction.h5) that amset interpolates (BoltzTraP2). Produced by VaspAmsetMaker's dense-uniform step. The map has no electronic band-structure node (BandGap is present, the full bands are not).

entries.name: amset-electrical-conductivity-electronic
entries.quantity: Electronic electrical conductivity sigma: the Onsager L0 coefficient. Full (doping, T, 3, 3) tensor.
entries.units: S/m (run.py:583 header, data.py:484 to_data unit 'S/m'). NOT S/cm.
entries.producing_api: solve_boltzman_transport_equation -> _calculate_transport_properties -> BoltzTraP2 calc_Onsager_coefficients (transport.py:36-39,186). Via VaspAmsetMaker in AtomisticSkills.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: Native S/m is the map canonical for conductivity. Trap: much AMSET literature and the GaAs README quote S/cm implicitly via mobility; 1 S/cm = 100 S/m. Register serving unit S/m explicitly.
entries.maps_to: none (new: ElectricalConductivity[carrier=electronic]). Shares the EXACT dimension of the config-thermo scan's IonicConductivity (M=-1,L=-3,T=3,I=2). See conductivity_family_proposal.

entries.name: amset-seebeck-coefficient
entries.quantity: Seebeck coefficient S (thermopower): Onsager L1/L0 ratio. Full (doping, T, 3, 3) tensor.
entries.units: microvolt per kelvin (muV/K); source multiplies raw V/K by 1e6 (transport.py:191), header 'S [uV/K]' (run.py:583).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: muV/K -> V/K is x1e-6. Sign carries the carrier type (n vs p). Register serving unit muV/K.
entries.maps_to: none (new: SeebeckCoefficient). First map node with I=-1 that is not Voltage; dimension (1,2,-3,-1,0,-1,0) VERIFIED.

entries.name: amset-electronic-thermal-conductivity
entries.quantity: Electronic thermal conductivity kappa_e: the electronic contribution to heat transport (Onsager L2 minus Peltier feedback). Full (doping, T, 3, 3) tensor.
entries.units: W/(m.K) (BoltzTraP2 Onsager). CAVEAT: amset source itself flags this: data.py:483 '# TODO: confirm unit of kappa', header unit '?' (data.py:484). W/(m.K) is the BoltzTraP2 convention; treat as unconfirmed-by-amset-doc but standard.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: W/(m.K) is the map canonical for thermal conductivity. MANDATORY: this is the ELECTRONIC contribution, distinct-by-carrier from the map's lattice ThermalConductivity[*] family; see lattice_vs_electronic_kappa. Same dimension, must NOT merge.
entries.maps_to: none (new: ThermalConductivity[carrier=electronic] or ElectronicThermalConductivity). Dimension identical to lattice ThermalConductivity (1,1,-3,-1,0,0,0); the distinction is the carrier label, MANDATORY to keep (see lattice_vs_electronic_kappa).

entries.name: amset-carrier-mobility
entries.quantity: Carrier mobility mu: sigma normalized by carrier charge density. Full (doping, T, 3, 3) tensor per mechanism, plus 'overall'.
entries.producing_api: _calculate_mobility (transport.py:73-137); separate_mobility gives per-mechanism breakdown (run.py:615-624). Only computed for non-metals (transport.py:47-49). Via VaspAmsetMaker.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: cm^2/(V.s) -> m^2/(V.s) is x1e-4. The per-mechanism decomposition is the resolved spectrum (which scattering channel limits mobility).
entries.maps_to: none (new: CarrierMobility). Dimension (M=-1,T=2,I=1) VERIFIED. Related to ElectricalConductivity by sigma = n e mu.

entries.name: amset-scattering-rates
entries.quantity: Per-mechanism carrier scattering rates 1/tau (momentum relaxation): the microscopic quantity amset adds over bare BoltzTraP2. Resolved per (spin, doping, T, band, k) and per mechanism (ADP/IMP/PIE/POP...).
entries.producing_api: amset.scattering.calculate; mechanisms ADP/IMP/PIE/POP/CRT/MFP/SRT (scattering/{elastic,inelastic,basic}.py). Matthiessen sum: total lifetime = 1/sum(rates) (transport.py:101,163).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: s^-1 is the map FREQUENCY canonical. Per-mechanism decomposition is the resolved spectrum layer; likely representation-only detail feeding the mobility/conductivity nodes rather than a top-level observable.
anchors: /tmp/amsetsrc amset-0.5.1 amset/plot/rates.py:22-26; amset/scattering/elastic.py:84,280,369; amset/scattering/inelastic.py:60; amset/scattering/basic.py:54,85,125; amset/core/transport.py:101,163
entries.maps_to: none. Microscopic scattering rates (per band/k) are the spectrum feeding the transport tensors; ingest via the representation/discretization layer, not as a standalone scalar node. Dimension = FREQUENCY (1/s).

entries.name: amset-deformation-potential
entries.package: amset (input; produced by VaspAmsetMaker strained-band step)
entries.quantity: Acoustic deformation potential (VBM and CBM band-edge shift per unit strain): an amset scattering INPUT, computed by the VASP strained-supercell step of VaspAmsetMaker.
entries.producing_api: VaspAmsetMaker deformation-potential job (SKILL.md 'deformation potential calculation'); amset.deformation.* interpolator (deformation/potentials.py).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV -> J is ev_to_hartree*hartree_to_joule (amset works in Hartree atomic units internally). ENERGY-dimensioned.
entries.maps_to: none (new: DeformationPotential, a source/input node; ENERGY dimension, but band-edge-strain-derivative physics distinct from TotalEnergy/FormationEnergy).

entries.name: amset-piezoelectric-constant
entries.quantity: Piezoelectric stress constant for PIE scattering: an amset scattering INPUT.
entries.producing_api: amset input; PIE mechanism (elastic.py:368-410). REVIEW: amset casts the input via cast_piezoelectric_tensor (util.py:146) from a 3x6 Voigt to the full 3x3x3 e-tensor, then contracts it with the inverse high-frequency dielectric to the h piezoelectric coefficient (elastic.py:398-406, einsum 'mn,mkl->nkl'). NOTE: piezoelectric_constant is NOT wired by atomate2 VaspAmsetMaker (no 'piezo' in vasp/flows/amset.py); PIE scattering requires the user to supply it via amset_settings. So for the GaAs skill default flow, PIE is only active if piezoelectric_constant is user-added.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: C/m^2 = A.s/m^2 = (L^-2, T, I). Same dimension as spontaneous polarization P_s (the VASP-scan ferroelectric candidate). Cross-domain overlap. The e-tensor is rank-3 (3x3x3), served in Voigt 3x6; the dimension is per-component C/m^2.
entries.maps_to: none (new: PiezoelectricConstant). Dimension C/m^2 = (0,-2,1,0,0,1,0), same as SpontaneousPolarization from the VASP scan; sibling in the polarization family.

conductivity_family_proposal.task: State whether ElectricalConductivity[carrier=electronic] vs IonicConductivity should be siblings, one labeled family, or distinct tags. Gather facts, propose, do not decide.
facts: amset's electronic sigma has dimension (M=-1, L=-3, T=3, I=2), VERIFIED from S/m (run.py:583, data.py:484).
The config-thermo scan's IonicConductivity (Nernst-Einstein, pymatgen-analysis-diffusion) has the IDENTICAL dimension (M=-1,L=-3,T=3,I=2), VERIFIED there by two hand derivations.
So electronic and ionic conductivity share the exponent vector EXACTLY: both are electrical conductivity S/m.

But the physics differs: electronic sigma is carried by band electrons/holes (Onsager L0 over a DFT band structure with scattering rates); ionic sigma is carried by mobile ions (Nernst-Einstein from tracer diffusivity, assuming Haven ratio 1). Different carrier, different producing operator, different domain (electronic-transport vs diffusion).
amset serves S/m; pymatgen-analysis-diffusion serves mS/cm. Unit disagreement within the family (1 S/m = 10 mS/cm).

PRECEDENT on the map: ThermalConductivity is ALREADY a labeled family carrying carrier/route distinctions via labels (bte_solver=rta, transport_model=wigner|green_kubo|nemd|..., registry.py LABEL_KEYS 'transport_model'). The map already distinguishes same-dimension conductivities by a label, not by separate quantity tags.

REVIEW: registry.py LABEL_KEYS currently holds {order, bte_solver, transport_model, channel, wrt} (omai/operator/registry.py:211-219). A new 'carrier' key is CLEAN: no name collision, and its values {electronic, ionic, lattice, hole, electron} do not overlap any existing key's frozenset (bte_solver/transport_model/channel/wrt are phonon-thermal-specific; order is an int-valued key). Adding 'carrier' is a pure additive registry change.
conductivity_family_proposal.proposal: Recommend ONE quantity tag 'electrical_conductivity' carrying a carrier label: ElectricalConductivity[carrier=electronic] (amset) and ElectricalConductivity[carrier=ionic] (the config-thermo IonicConductivity). Rationale: they share the exact dimension AND the physical quantity (charge transport, S/m); they differ only in which particle carries the charge, which is exactly what a label key exists for (mirroring the ThermalConductivity[transport_model=...] pattern). This would RENAME the config-thermo scan's proposed IonicConductivity to ElectricalConductivity[carrier=ionic]. ALTERNATIVE (weaker): keep IonicConductivity as its own tag and mint ElectricalConductivity for the electronic one as siblings-by-tag; this loses the shared-quantity insight and risks a false non-merge. DECISION DEFERRED to the orchestrator. A new LABEL_KEY 'carrier' with values {electronic, ionic} (and later {hole, electron} refinements) would be the registry change. Note the serving-unit split (S/m vs mS/cm) must be recorded per-representation regardless of which topology wins.

lattice_vs_electronic_kappa.mandate: The atomate2-vasp scan verdict and this task both mark the lattice-vs-electronic thermal-conductivity distinction MANDATORY to keep.
facts: amset's kappa_e (electronic_thermal_conductivity, data.py:80,419,473) is the ELECTRONIC contribution to thermal conductivity, from the Onsager coefficients over the electron/hole band structure.

The map's ThermalConductivity[*] family (11 nodes: bte_solver=rta|direct_inverse, transport_model=wigner*|qhgk|green_kubo|nemd|hnemd) is the LATTICE (phonon) thermal conductivity, from the phonon BTE / MD heat current.
Both have dimension (M=1, L=1, T=-3, Th=-1, 0, 0, 0) = W/(m.K), IDENTICAL (dimensions.py:115).

The total measured thermal conductivity of a material is kappa_total = kappa_lattice + kappa_electronic; they are ADDITIVE contributions from different carriers. Merging them (or letting them false-merge on shared dimension) would be a physics error: they are produced by different operators in different domains and answer different questions.

amset source itself is unsure of kappa's unit (data.py:483 TODO), so the electronic kappa should be registered with an explicit W/(m.K) unit assertion and a 'confirm against BoltzTraP2' provenance note.
lattice_vs_electronic_kappa.conclusion: Register the electronic thermal conductivity with a carrier label that keeps it distinct from the lattice family: either ThermalConductivity[carrier=electronic] (parallel to the proposed carrier label on electrical conductivity) OR a distinct ElectronicThermalConductivity tag. The map's existing ThermalConductivity family is implicitly carrier=lattice; if a carrier label is introduced, the existing 11 nodes gain carrier=lattice for symmetry. Same dimension, MANDATORY distinct identity. Same structural pattern as the conductivity_family_proposal above (a carrier label over a shared dimension).

unit_factors.conductivity_S_per_m_vs_S_per_cm: amset serves sigma in S/m (run.py:583, data.py:484). Common AMSET usage and the config-thermo IonicConductivity serve S/cm and mS/cm respectively. 1 S/cm = 100 S/m; 1 mS/cm = 0.1 S/m. Register amset's S/m explicitly to avoid a 100x (or 1000x vs mS/cm) trap.
unit_factors.seebeck_muV_per_K: amset multiplies the raw V/K by 1e6 to serve muV/K (transport.py:191). 1 muV/K = 1e-6 V/K.
unit_factors.pop_frequency_THz: pop_frequency input in THz (defaults.yaml:41); internally *1e12*2pi/s_to_au to angular frequency in atomic units (inelastic.py:92). THz is linear frequency nu, the 2pi makes omega.
unit_factors.elastic_constant_GPa: elastic_constant input in GPa (defaults.yaml:36). REVIEW CORRECTION: it is the FULL rank-4 elastic tensor, not a longitudinal scalar. cast_elastic_tensor (util.py:115) expands a scalar/Voigt/full input to the (3,3,3,3) C_ijkl; c_long is derived per q-direction via the Christoffel construction at run time (elastic.py:180-183,266-267). VaspAmsetMaker wires the full elastic.output.elastic_tensor.raw (atomate2 vasp/flows/amset.py:290).
unit_factors.internal_atomic_units: amset works internally in Hartree atomic units (constants.py: bohr, hartree, au_to_s); all serving-unit conversions happen at output. Cross-code EXPECTED_AGREE must use the serving units above.

traps: CONDUCTIVITY UNIT: amset serves sigma in S/m, NOT S/cm. Header 'sigma [S/m]' (run.py:583), to_data 'S/m' (data.py:484). 1 S/cm = 100 S/m. The config-thermo IonicConductivity serves mS/cm. Same family, three different serving units in the wild (S/m, S/cm, mS/cm).

KAPPA UNIT UNCONFIRMED BY AMSET: data.py:483 '# TODO: confirm unit of kappa', header unit '?' (data.py:484). The electronic thermal conductivity is W/(m.K) by BoltzTraP2 convention, but amset's own output does not assert it. Flag on the representation.

LATTICE vs ELECTRONIC KAPPA: kappa_e (amset) and kappa_lattice (phonon domain) share the exact dimension W/(m.K) = (1,1,-3,-1,0,0,0). MANDATORY distinct identity by carrier; total kappa is their sum. Never merge on shared dimension.
ELECTRONIC vs IONIC CONDUCTIVITY: share the exact dimension (M=-1,L=-3,T=3,I=2) = S/m. Different carrier, different domain. Propose carrier label; do not false-merge, do not false-split.
SEEBECK muV/K: amset multiplies by 1e6 (transport.py:191). Sign carries carrier type.

MOBILITY only for non-metals: _calculate_mobility refuses on metals (transport.py:47-49); sigma/seebeck/kappa are still computed. Per-mechanism mobility breakdown requires separate_mobility (default True).

PER-MECHANISM RATE DECOMPOSITION: total scattering is the Matthiessen sum 1/tau_total = sum_mech 1/tau_mech (transport.py:101,163). Per-mechanism rates and per-mechanism mobilities are the resolved spectrum; the transport tensors are the sum. Do not double-count.
DOPING SIGN: positive = p-type, negative = n-type (defaults.yaml:8). Opposite of the naive 'negative charge = electrons = negative' intuition. Input in cm^-3, internally bohr^-3.
TRANSPORT TENSORS ARE (doping, T, 3, 3): not scalars. The GaAs single-number mobility is a tensor_average at one (doping, T). Ingest the full grid via the representation layer.

eps_0 vs eps_inf: amset needs BOTH static_dielectric (eps_0, ion-clamped + ionic) AND high_frequency_dielectric (eps_inf). The map's DielectricTensor is eps_inf only. The static eps_0 is a distinct quantity amset requires (POP/PIE/IMP screening).

VERSION: amset 0.5.1 (pip download 2026-07-10); AtomisticSkills atomate2-agent env pins amset with no version (SKILL.md constraints). Pin at encode; the '# TODO confirm kappa unit' may be fixed upstream in a later version.

INDIRECT USE: AtomisticSkills never calls the amset Python API directly; it goes through atomate2 VaspAmsetMaker and reads the amset JSON output. The output contract (amset transport JSON: doping, T, Fermi_level, then sigma/seebeck/kappa/mobility tensors) is the ground truth, anchored to amset/core/data.py:to_data.
open_questions: Conductivity family topology: ElectricalConductivity[carrier=electronic|ionic] as ONE labeled family (recommended, mirrors ThermalConductivity[transport_model=...]), vs distinct IonicConductivity/ElectricalConductivity tags. Would rename the config-thermo IonicConductivity. Needs a 'carrier' LABEL_KEY. DECISION DEFERRED.

Electronic thermal conductivity identity: ThermalConductivity[carrier=electronic] (adds carrier=lattice to the existing 9 lattice nodes) vs a distinct ElectronicThermalConductivity tag. Same dimension as lattice kappa, MANDATORY distinct.

Seebeck as a standalone node vs part of a thermoelectric bundle (sigma, S, kappa_e, and derived power factor S^2 sigma / figure of merit ZT). amset computes sigma, S, kappa_e; power factor and ZT are downstream. Mint Seebeck now (dimension verified), ZT later.
Scattering rates: representation-only (per band/k spectrum feeding the tensors) confirmed, or a top-level ScatteringRate node? Recommend representation-only.

compute_electronic_transport edge: a genuinely cross-domain operator whose inputs are DielectricTensor (eps_inf) + a new static-eps_0 node + Frequency-derived pop_frequency + the FULL ElasticConstants rank-4 tensor (review correction: amset consumes the whole tensor, not a reduced scalar) + new DeformationPotential + new PiezoelectricConstant (NOT wired by VaspAmsetMaker; user-supplied via amset_settings if PIE is needed) + a VASP dense band structure. Which inputs are edges from existing nodes vs new source nodes? DielectricTensor, Frequency (->pop_frequency), ElasticConstants, BornCharges (provenance, via calculate_polar_phonon_frequency) already on map; deformation_potential, piezoelectric_constant, static eps_0, band structure are new.

static dielectric eps_0 vs the map's DielectricTensor (eps_inf): mint a StaticDielectricTensor (ionic + electronic) distinct from the electronic eps_inf, since amset needs both. The VASP scan already separated eps_inf (LEPSILON) from the frequency-dependent eps(omega) (LOPTICS); eps_0 is a third distinct dielectric quantity.
PiezoelectricConstant (C/m^2) shares the dimension of SpontaneousPolarization (VASP-scan candidate); one polarization family or distinct?
kappa unit: amset's '# TODO: confirm unit of kappa' (data.py:483) - confirm W/(m.K) against BoltzTraP2 bandlib source before EXPECTED_AGREE.
amset version pin: 0.5.1 downloaded; atomate2-agent env unpinned. Pin before an encode relies on the to_data output schema.

review_verdicts_2026-07-10.method: Four transport dimensions re-derived independently in pure Python AND cross-checked against omai.operator.dimensions.VOLTAGE/THERMAL_CONDUCTIVITY/FREQUENCY. amset 0.5.1 source re-read at /tmp/amsetsrc/amset_pkg (data.py, transport.py, run.py, defaults.yaml, util.py, scattering/*). atomate2 0.1.4 VaspAmsetMaker re-read at /tmp/a2src/extracted/atomate2/vasp/flows/amset.py. config-thermo IonicConductivity dimension re-read from scans/config-thermo-atomistic-skills.json. registry.py LABEL_KEYS re-read. map/log.jsonl and docs/data/graph.json re-read for freshness.

dimension_verdicts.seebeck (1,2,-3,-1,0,-1,0): VERIFIED. Pure-python (V/K from omai VOLTAGE minus Th) and hand agree.
dimension_verdicts.mobility (-1,0,2,0,0,1,0): VERIFIED. L^2/(V.s) = M^-1 T^2 I. Pure-python and hand agree.
dimension_verdicts.electronic sigma (-1,-3,3,0,0,2,0): VERIFIED. S/m = (A/V)/m. Identical exponent vector to config-thermo IonicConductivity (re-confirmed from that catalog line 313).
dimension_verdicts.electronic kappa (1,1,-3,-1,0,0,0): VERIFIED. W/(m.K). Identical to omai THERMAL_CONDUCTIVITY object exponents.

review_verdicts_2026-07-10.to_data_unit_findings: CONFIRMED verbatim at amset/core/data.py: to_data defined line 461; the comment '# TODO: confirm unit of kappa' is line 483 verbatim; line 484 is `for prop, unit in [("cond", "S/m"), ("seebeck", "µV/K"), ("kappa", "?")]:`. So SERIALIZED units are sigma=S/m (confirmed, NOT S/cm), seebeck=µV/K (micro-sign U+00B5; the catalog's ASCII 'muV/K' is a faithful transliteration), kappa='?' (unconfirmed by amset itself). Tensor shape (n_doping, n_temperature, 3, 3) with to_data writing the upper-triangle xx,xy,xz,yy,yz,zz per (doping,T) row via np.triu_indices(3) (line 464): CONFIRMED. run.py:583 header uses Unicode glyphs 'σ [S/m]', 'S [µV/K]', 'μ [cm²/Vs]': CONFIRMED, sigma is S/m.

entries.amset-electrical-conductivity-electronic: VERDICT: CONFIRMED. Dimension (-1,-3,3,0,0,2,0) verified. Serving unit S/m verified verbatim (data.py:484, run.py:583). new-node-candidate stands. Exponent-vector identity with config-thermo IonicConductivity RE-CONFIRMED.
entries.amset-seebeck-coefficient: VERDICT: CONFIRMED. Dimension (1,2,-3,-1,0,-1,0) verified (first non-Voltage I=-1 map node). Serving unit µV/K verified (transport.py:191 seebeck*=1e6; data.py:484). new-node-candidate stands.
entries.amset-electronic-thermal-conductivity: VERDICT: CONFIRMED. Dimension (1,1,-3,-1,0,0,0) verified (= omai THERMAL_CONDUCTIVITY). Unit UNCONFIRMED-BY-AMSET flag verified verbatim (data.py:483-484 '?'). MANDATORY distinct-by-carrier from lattice kappa stands. new-node-candidate stands.
entries.amset-carrier-mobility: VERDICT: CONFIRMED. Dimension (-1,0,2,0,0,1,0) verified. cm^2/(V.s) verified (transport.py:133-135). Non-metal guard verified (transport.py:47-48 'refusing to calculate carrier mobility'). Per-mechanism breakdown via separate_mobility verified (run.py:615-624). new-node-candidate stands.
entries.amset-scattering-rates: VERDICT: CONFIRMED. FREQUENCY (0,0,-1,0,0,0,0) = 1/s (plot/rates.py:22-26 unit 's$^{-1}$'). Matthiessen sum verified (transport.py:101,163 '1/np.sum(scattering_rates)'). representation-only stands.
entries.amset-deformation-potential: VERDICT: CONFIRMED. ENERGY (1,2,-2,0,0,0,0), eV (defaults.yaml:37 comment 'in eV, given for the VBM and CBM'). Produced by VaspAmsetMaker DeformationPotentialMaker (vasp/flows/amset.py:62-135, deformation.h5). new-node-candidate (source) stands.
entries.amset-piezoelectric-constant: VERDICT: CONFIRMED with two ADDITIONS. Dimension C/m^2 (0,-2,1,0,0,1,0) verified, same as SpontaneousPolarization. ADDITION 1: the input is the full rank-3 e-tensor (3x6 Voigt -> 3x3x3, util.py:146 cast_piezoelectric_tensor), contracted with inverse eps_inf to the h-coefficient (elastic.py:398-406). ADDITION 2: piezoelectric_constant is NOT wired by VaspAmsetMaker (no piezo in flows/amset.py); user must supply it for PIE. new-node-candidate stands.

physics_changing_corrections: ELASTIC INPUT (was WRONG): the catalog repeatedly called elastic_constant 'a single longitudinal-average scalar, a reduction of the rank-4 tensor'. FALSE. amset consumes the FULL rank-4 (3,3,3,3) elastic tensor: cast_elastic_tensor (util.py:115) expands any scalar/Voigt/full input to (3,3,3,3); c_long is derived per q-direction at run time via the Christoffel construction (get_christoffel_tensors einsum + solve_christoffel_equation, elastic.py:180-183,266-267). atomate2 VaspAmsetMaker wires elastic.output.elastic_tensor.raw (the full raw tensor, vasp/flows/amset.py:290). A scalar input is only an isotropic-tensor convenience. The cross-domain edge is the full ElasticConstants tensor. CORRECTED in cross_domain_inputs, unit_factors, open_questions.

BORN-CHARGE WIRING (sharpened, was under-specified): VaspAmsetMaker reduces Born effective charges + normal modes into the effective pop_frequency via calculate_polar_phonon_frequency(structure, normalmode_frequencies, normalmode_eigenvecs, outcar['born']) (vasp/flows/amset.py:245-250). The catalog's 'upstream provenance' claim is CORRECT and now anchored precisely.
PIEZO NOT AUTO-WIRED (added): PIE scattering is not automatic in the skill's default GaAs flow; VaspAmsetMaker does not compute or pass piezoelectric_constant.
THERMALCONDUCTIVITY FAMILY COUNT (was WRONG): catalog said 11 lattice nodes; the current graph has 9 (2 bte_solver + 7 transport_model). CORRECTED.
non_physics_corrections: Graph freshness: map/log.jsonl is 144 records; matcalc/ASE encode did NOT land (no records 145-147); graph is 73 nodes. review_snapshot_2026-07-10 added.
registry 'carrier' LABEL_KEY: no collision. Current keys {order, bte_solver, transport_model, channel, wrt}; a 'carrier' key is a clean additive change.
orchestrator_decisions: MINT the four electronic-transport output nodes (ElectricalConductivity[carrier=electronic], SeebeckCoefficient, ThermalConductivity[carrier=electronic] or ElectronicThermalConductivity, CarrierMobility); all four dimensions independently verified. Register the three NEW dimension constants the map lacks: electrical_conductivity (-1,-3,3,0,0,2,0), seebeck_coefficient (1,2,-3,-1,0,-1,0), mobility (-1,0,2,0,0,1,0). electronic kappa reuses THERMAL_CONDUCTIVITY.

CONDUCTIVITY FAMILY: recommended topology is ONE 'electrical_conductivity' tag with a carrier label; this RENAMES config-thermo IonicConductivity to ElectricalConductivity[carrier=ionic]. DECISION DEFERRED to orchestrator but the exponent-vector identity is now double-confirmed and the 'carrier' LABEL_KEY is collision-free.
KAPPA CARRIER LABEL is MANDATORY (never merge electronic and lattice kappa on shared dimension). If a carrier label is introduced, the 9 existing lattice nodes gain carrier=lattice.
ELASTIC EDGE: draw the cross-domain edge from the FULL ElasticConstants tensor node (not a reduced scalar) to the electronic-transport operator.
eps_0: mint a StaticDielectricTensor distinct from the eps_inf DielectricTensor (amset needs both; VaspAmsetMaker computes eps_0 = eps_inf + eps_ionic, flows/amset.py:261-266).
kappa unit: record W/(m.K) with an explicit 'confirm against BoltzTraP2, amset serializes '?'' provenance note before any EXPECTED_AGREE.
PIN amset at encode (0.5.1 here; the kappa-unit '# TODO' may be resolved upstream).
